238.reason: [Error type "a" should be reported only once per invalid single '&' or '|', but the current logic reports it and continues processing; however, the real issue is that after reporting the error, the function returns without consuming the next token properly in all contexts. More critically, when a single '&' is encountered, 'content' only contains "&", but after input.unget(), the next call to next_token will re-read the same '&' again, causing an infinite loop.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Error type "a" should be reported only once per invalid single '&' or '|', but the current logic reports it and continues processing; however, the real issue is that after reporting the error, the function returns without consuming the next token properly in all contexts. More critically, when a single '&' is encountered, 'content' only contains "&", but after input.unget(), the next call to next_token will re-read the same '&' again, causing an infinite loop.]

248.reason: [Same issue as above: after input.unget(), the standalone '|' remains in the stream, and the next call to next_token will read it again, leading to an infinite loop of error reporting on the same character.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: after input.unget(), the standalone '|' remains in the stream, and the next call to next_token will read it again, leading to an infinite loop of error reporting on the same character.]
